mnemonics.title: S.M.A.C.K. for Shared-Memory Challenges
mnemonics.content: Synchronization (complexity), Memory bandwidth (bottleneck), Address space (global), Coherence (cache overhead), K(ost) for large systems.

mnemonics.title: D.I.S.C.O. for Distributed-Memory Challenges
mnemonics.content: Data partitioning (required), Interconnect (network dependency), Sending messages (explicit), Complexity (programming), Overhead (communication latency).

mnemonics.title: UMA vs NUMA
mnemonics.content: UMA = Uniform = all processors see Same access time. NUMA = Non-Uniform = Near memory is fast, far memory is slow. Think: UMA is 'U' for 'Universal' speed, NUMA is 'N' for 'Nearby is faster'.

mnemonics.title: Programming Model Pairing
mnemonics.content: S.O. and D.M. -- Shared memory uses OpenMP (S.O.), Distributed memory uses MPI (D.M.). Remember: Share with Open arms (OpenMP), Distribute by Mailing Packages (MPI).

mnemonics.title: Hybrid = MPI + OpenMP
mnemonics.content: Modern supercomputers are Hybrid: MPI for Messages between Nodes, OpenMP for threads within a Node. Think: MPI is the Postal service between houses, OpenMP is people talking inside the same house.
